items.title: Stephen Hawking's black hole laws just got a major upgrade
items.summary: Scientists have developed a new framework that could finally apply the laws of thermodynamics to real, ever-changing black holes instead of only perfectly stable ones. The advance may improve our understanding of black hole mergers, evaporation, and the powerful gravitational wave events detected by

items.title: Scientists discovered the brain doesn't make decisions the way we thought
items.summary: A new study suggests the brain begins making decisions much earlier than scientists previously thought. Researchers found that even primary sensory regions are influenced by higher brain areas through rapid feedback loops, rather than simply passing information forward. This more dynamic view of bra

items.title: Popular weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy may slow biological aging
items.summary: Researchers found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, slowed biological aging markers in adults with HIV, marking the first clinical evidence that the drug may influence human aging. Although the findings are encouraging, scientists say larger studies are needed before con

items.title: NASA’s Roman Telescope Will Spot Distant Black Holes That Shred Stars
items.summary: Lee esta nota de prensa en español aquí. How do black holes at the center of galaxies form and grow over time? items.title: A 200-year-old physics experiment could help build future computers
items.summary: Scientists at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore have discovered a surprisingly simple way to create exotic light structures called optical skyrmions using a 200-year-old optical effect known as the Poisson spot.
